exercises.title: Which test slice for each scenario?
exercises.scenario: Each test slice loads different components.
exercises.instructions:
Match each testing need to the correct Spring Boot test approach:

A. Test that GET /api/products returns 200 with JSON array
B. Test that findByCategory('Electronics') returns 2 products from H2
C. Test that ProductService.getTotalValue() calculates correctly (no Spring needed)
D. Test full CRUD: POST, then GET, then DELETE with real HTTP
E. Test that POST with blank name returns 400 with field error
exercises.expected_solution:
A → @WebMvcTest
B → @DataJpaTest
C → Plain JUnit + Mockito (no Spring)
D → @SpringBootTest RANDOM_PORT
E → @WebMvcTest

test_cases.expected:
A → @WebMvcTest
B → @DataJpaTest
C → Plain JUnit + Mockito (no Spring)
D → @SpringBootTest RANDOM_PORT
E → @WebMvcTest

hints: Pure business logic needs no Spring context

exercises.title: Complete a @WebMvcTest
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks to complete this @WebMvcTest.

blanks.hint: The HTTP test client injected by @WebMvcTest
Creates a Mockito mock in the Spring context

exercises.title: Minimal @WebMvcTest — GET all products
exercises.scenario: Test GET /api/products returns 200 and JSON array.
exercises.instructions: Write a @WebMvcTest for `ProductController.getAll()`. Mock the service to return 2 products. Assert 200 status and array length 2.

exercises.title: @WebMvcTest — GET by ID with 200 and 404
exercises.scenario: Test both found and not-found scenarios for GET /{id}.
exercises.instructions:
Write 2 @WebMvcTest tests for `GET /api/products/{id}`:
1. Service returns product → 200 with name in JSON
2. Service throws ResourceNotFoundException → 404
hints: GlobalExceptionHandler must also be loaded by @WebMvcTest (it is auto-detected)

exercises.title: @WebMvcTest — POST validation 400
exercises.scenario: Test that invalid POST returns 400 with field errors.
exercises.instructions:
Write 2 @WebMvcTest tests for `POST /api/products`:
1. Valid request → 201, response has id and name
2. Blank name → 400 with 'name' key in error body
hints: jsonPath('$.name').exists() checks the field is present in error response

exercises.title: @DataJpaTest — save and find round-trip
exercises.scenario: @DataJpaTest loads H2 + JPA for repository testing.
exercises.instructions:
Write @DataJpaTest tests for `OrderRepository`:
1. save → auto-assigns id
2. findById after save → returns saved entity
3. findByStatus('PENDING') → 2 of 3 seeded orders
4. deleteById → entity no longer exists
hints: @BeforeEach seed: data saved before each test, rolled back after
Each test starts fresh due to @DataJpaTest rollback
findByStatus: 2 PENDING from seed + any saved in the test

exercises.title: @DataJpaTest with @Import for service testing
exercises.scenario: @Import loads a service bean into the DataJpa slice.
exercises.instructions: Write @DataJpaTest tests for a `ProductService` that depends on `ProductRepository`. Use @Import to load the service. Test: create (success), create duplicate (throws), delete (removes from DB).
hints: existsByName: checks for duplicate before save

exercises.title: MockMvc jsonPath assertions — complex JSON
exercises.scenario: jsonPath() navigates nested and array JSON responses.
exercises.instructions:
Write @WebMvcTest tests asserting on complex JSON:
1. GET /api/products returns array with 3 items, first item name='Mouse'
2. GET /api/products/P001 returns nested JSON: id, name, price, AND nested category object with id and name
3. GET /api/products?category=Electronics returns 2-item array
hints: jsonPath('$[0].name'): first array element's name field

exercises.title: @SpringBootTest NONE — service layer integration
exercises.scenario: @SpringBootTest(webEnvironment=NONE) tests the service layer with real Spring context.
exercises.instructions:
Write @SpringBootTest(webEnvironment=NONE) tests for `InventoryService`:
1. @Autowired the service (no mock)
2. Test addItem and getStock
3. Test lowStock alert
4. No HTTP involved — pure service test with Spring context
hints: webEnvironment=NONE: loads full Spring context but no Tomcat
@Autowired real service bean — not a mock
Useful for testing @Value config, @PostConstruct, @Transactional in real Spring context

exercises.title: @DataJpaTest rollback behavior
exercises.scenario: Each @Test in @DataJpaTest runs in a rollback transaction.
exercises.expected_solution:
Count in test2: 0
Reason: @DataJpaTest wraps each test in a transaction that rolls back.
The product saved in test1 is rolled back — test2 sees an empty database.

test_cases.expected:
Count in test2: 0
Reason: @DataJpaTest wraps each test in a transaction that rolls back.
The product saved in test1 is rolled back — test2 sees an empty database.

hints: @DataJpaTest: each @Test is in a @Transactional that rolls back after
test1 saves but the transaction never commits

exercises.title: @SpringBootTest RANDOM_PORT — full integration
exercises.scenario: Full stack: real HTTP client → real controller → real DB.
exercises.instructions:
Write @SpringBootTest(RANDOM_PORT) integration tests for the Product API:
1. POST creates a product → 201
2. GET by created id → 200 with correct name
3. DELETE the created product → 204
4. GET deleted product → 404

Use TestRestTemplate. Clean DB in @BeforeEach.
hints: TestRestTemplate: doesn't throw on 4xx — use getStatusCode() to check
Use LinkedHashMap.class as response type when Product type may not be accessible

exercises.title: Full controller test suite — happy path + all errors
exercises.instructions:
Write a complete @WebMvcTest for `OrderController` with 6 tests:
1. POST valid order → 201 with id
2. POST blank userId → 400 with 'userId' error
3. POST negative price → 400 with 'price' error
4. GET by id found → 200 with order data
5. GET by id not found → 404 with error JSON
6. DELETE by id → 204
hints: validOrder() helper method: reduces duplication across tests
For negative price: Map.of() is immutable — use HashMap and put()
doNothing().when(service).deleteOrder() not needed: void methods don't throw by default

exercises.title: Three-slice test strategy — same API
exercises.scenario: Write all three test types for the same Product API.
exercises.instructions:
Write one test each for all three slices testing the Product API:
1. @WebMvcTest: POST returns 201 with correct JSON
2. @DataJpaTest: findByCategory derived query returns correct count
3. @SpringBootTest NONE: @Autowired ProductService, create product, verify persisted

All 3 tests must pass.
exercises.starter_code: // Three test slices for the same Product API
hints: Three independent test classes, each targeting a different layer
@SpringBootTest NONE: no HTTP, full Spring context with DB

exercises.title: @WebMvcTest — test DELETE and PUT
exercises.scenario: Test all HTTP methods including DELETE (204/404) and PUT (200/404).
exercises.instructions:
Write 4 @WebMvcTest tests:
1. DELETE existing → 204 No Content
2. DELETE non-existing → 404
3. PUT valid update → 200 with updated fields
4. PUT non-existing → 404

exercises.title: Complete test pyramid — Module 13 synthesis
exercises.scenario: Full test pyramid for the Zerodha order API.
exercises.instructions:
Build a complete test pyramid for the stock order API:
1. Plain Mockito test for OrderService.calculateTotal (no Spring)
2. @WebMvcTest: POST valid order 201, POST invalid symbol 400, GET not-found 404
3. @DataJpaTest: findByUserId returns user's orders, countByStatus aggregation
4. @SpringBootTest RANDOM_PORT: POST order and GET it back end-to-end

All tests must pass. Minimum: 7 test methods total.
hints: Layer 1: plain JUnit + Mockito → no Spring annotations needed
